chunks.text: Antoni Gaudi, from the end of the nineteenth century through the early twentieth
was the architect who created a unique architecture in Barcelona, Spain.
His most famous work is the cathedral called La Sagrada Familia.
For more than a hundred years, its construction has been continuing.
Gaudi found the inspiration for his design in nature.
Every column, every curve, was designed from a form found in nature.
He once said of his work, " I studied beside nature.
In 1926, Gaudi was hit by a tram on the street and died.
The cathedral, however, is still being built, century after century
through the donation from around the world.
Perhaps the fact of being unfinished speaks for itself of the building's historic value.
